headlines.title: US launches second night of strikes against Iran after ship struck by drone
headlines.snippet: The attacks come after an Iranian drone allegedly struck another commercial vessel in the Strait of Hormuz on Saturday.

headlines.snippet: Videos show Israeli settlers trying to seize a house under construction in the occupied West Bank.

headlines.title: Donald Trump nominates ex-state trooper Lance Schroyer as ICE director
headlines.snippet: The immigration enforcement agency has been without a Senate-confirmed director since 2017, during Trump's first term.

headlines.snippet: Vucic is under ⁠pressure after months ⁠of antigovernment protests.

headlines.title: Another powerful magnitude 4.8 earthquake hits near Venezuela
headlines.snippet: The latest quake comes as rescuers are still searching for survivors of the twin 7.2 and 7.5 earthquakes on Wednesday.

headlines.title: Lebanon says Trump backed ceasefire deal in call with Lebanese president
headlines.snippet: Lebanon says Trump backed ceasefire deal in call with Lebanese president Lebanese President Joseph Aoun says US President Donald Trump has reaffirmed Washington’s support for a Lebanon-Israel ceasefire agreement.

headlines.title: Oman weighs Strait of Hormuz fees after war
headlines.snippet: Oman weighs Strait of Hormuz fees after war Oman has told European officials that the Strait of Hormuz cannot simply return to the way it operated before the war, Bloomberg reports.

headlines.title: Trump congratulates Lebanese president on Israel framework deal
headlines.snippet: Trump congratulates Lebanese president on Israel framework deal US President Donald Trump has congratulated Lebanese President Joseph Aoun on signing a framework agreement with Israel, Axios has reported, citing officials.

headlines.title: Israel’s far-right finance minister boasts of army’s destruction of Lebanese infrastructure
headlines.snippet: Israel’s far-right finance minister boasts of army’s destruction of Lebanese infrastructure Far-right Israeli Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich has defended Israel’s agreement with Lebanon by openly saying the Israeli military continues to control territory inside the country.

headlines.title: Gaza rescue teams start new phase of operation to recover bodies
headlines.snippet: Gaza rescue teams have commenced a new phase of their operation to recover bodies. They plan to conclude this operation within three months as families continue their search for loved ones.

headlines.title: Iraq arrests protesters as unrest spreads over power cuts
headlines.snippet: Iraqi security forces arrested protesters in Kut following demonstrations against chronic power shortages. Unrest has spread across southern Iraq due to these power issues.

headlines.title: Israel settlers seizing Palestinian land to open 'tourist' sites
headlines.snippet: Israeli settlers have been seizing Palestinian land, converting an archaeological site near Fasayil into a tourism destination. This action is part of several incidents where land is being repurposed.

headlines.title: Israeli strike kills Palestinian siblings in Gaza tent camp
headlines.snippet: An Israeli drone strike in southern Gaza resulted in the deaths of two Palestinian siblings, one of whom was a 15-year-old girl. At least seven other individuals were wounded in the attack.

headlines.title: Palestinian parties could hold key to Israel's next government
headlines.snippet: New polling suggests that neither Benjamin Netanyahu's coalition nor the Zionist opposition can form a government without the support of Arab parties in Israel. This indicates a potentially crucial role for these parties.

headlines.snippet: The idea of a Syrian solution for Lebanon is presented as a delusion. This analysis was published by L'Orient Today.

headlines.title: European heat wave brings in cool cash for Asian air-conditioner makers as sales surge
headlines.snippet: European heat waves are driving a surge in air conditioner sales for Asian manufacturers like Samsung Electronics and China's Midea. As Europe experiences record temperatures, companies in South Korea, China, and Japan are seeing increased demand for cooling appliances.

headlines.title: Harry and Meghan accept King’s invitation to stay in royal residence during upcoming UK visit
headlines.snippet: Prince Harry and Meghan have accepted an invitation from the King to stay in a royal residence during their upcoming visit to the United Kingdom. This visit, their first in four years, sparks hopes for improved relations within the royal family.

headlines.title: A small plane slammed into the tallest skyscraper in China’s capital. Hours later, it was like nothing had happened.
headlines.snippet: A small plane crashed into Beijing's tallest skyscraper, the CITIC Tower, killing the pilot and injuring 13 others. Debris from the crash fell hundreds of feet, impacting the surrounding area.

headlines.title: The US and Iran have a deal on paper. At sea, the Strait of Hormuz is ‘chaotic’
headlines.snippet: Despite a US-Iran deal on paper, the Strait of Hormuz remains 'chaotic,' with Iran's Revolutionary Guards issuing warnings to ships. Iran has warned vessels transiting the vital oil chokepoint about consequences if they deviate from designated routes.

headlines.title: Egypt’s envoy to UN: Sudan bears consequences of ‘manufactured crisis’
headlines.snippet: Egypt's envoy to the UN stated that Sudan is facing the consequences of a 'manufactured crisis.' Ambassador Ehab Mostafa Awad Mostafa urged the international community not to remain passive regarding the suffering of the Sudanese people.

headlines.snippet: Tehran is preparing to host a large ceremony to commemorate the martyred Leader Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei. The event expects significant participation from Iranians and international guests.
